feat: add _HIDDEN_XLARGE for H200 (min h=512) to maximize GPU utilization

H200 NVL (140GB) was using _HIDDEN_LARGE which includes h=256 models
(~656K params, epoch 0.1s) → GPU at 4% utilization.
_HIDDEN_XLARGE enforces minimum h=512 for xlarge tier so H200 stays
at 100% GPU utilization with 30-70M parameter models.

commit.message:
fix: unset CLAUDECODE env vars before spawning claude -p subprocesses

When the pipeline runs inside a Claude Code session, the child
claude -p processes inherit CLAUDECODE=1 and CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT,
causing them to fail with recursive-run detection. Strip these from
the environment in both run_claude() and run_reviewer().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add project duplication feature

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: sidebar Contabilidad button now navigates to /admin/contabilidad

The parent items with children (like Contabilidad) were preventing
navigation via e.preventDefault() — clicking only toggled the submenu.
Now clicking navigates AND toggles the submenu. Fixed for both desktop
(ArturiaButton) and mobile (MobileNavRow) by replacing onClick/onNavigate
with onToggle/onNavigate pattern that doesn't block navigation.

commit.message:
Calculate deviation from range boundary (84-90°) not midpoint

Deviation now shows distance from nearest edge of normal range
instead of from 87°. E.g., mLDFA 93° = 3° deviation (from 90°),
not 6° (from 87°).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Postgres migration: use DEFAULT FALSE instead of DEFAULT 0

The ALTER TABLE migration for is_completed used DEFAULT 0 which is
invalid for PostgreSQL boolean columns. This caused the migration to
fail silently, so the column was never added. SQLAlchemy then crashed
on every SELECT (referencing a non-existent column), and the error
handler returned an empty list — making all chapters disappear.

commit.message:
chore: bump version to v1.3.0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: WDM Kernel Streaming diagnostic — how deep can user mode go?

Explores the KS filter layer below WASAPI from user mode. Enumerates
HDA filter graph, inspects pins/formats/instances, creates WaveRT pin
instances. Hits the wall at DMA buffer allocation (kernel-mode PortCls
interface only). Documents the exact boundary between user-mode and
kernel-mode audio on modern Windows.

Key finding: WASAPI exclusive IS the user-mode floor for WaveRT audio.
Going lower requires a kernel driver (like ASIO4ALL's ASIOA4A.sys).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove partner_bid_confidence + diagnostic report updates (#538)

partner_bid_confidence = partner_bid_level / 10, carrying zero new
information. OLS compensated with a 12.85 weight (10x partner_bid_level's
1.29). This redundancy contributed to feature fragility in the full arm.

Also fixes 3 errors in h2h_suit_regression_diagnostic.md:
- JSONL field: contract_type → contract (matches HandEndRecord schema)
- Log level: full → trick (valid LogLevel enum values)
- Training data path: stale data/training/r1/ → correct data/runs/ path

Adds 3 new hypotheses (H6 training sparsity, H7 base model shift,
H8 threshold paradox) and 2 new investigations (G, H) to diagnostic.

Feature count: 42 (39 hand + 3 partner)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: batch fixes 2026-03-05 (#375)

* fix: use Playwright fill() instead of native value setter in search smoke test (ROK-692)

The native value setter + input event dispatch trick races with React's
reconciliation in CI, causing the controlled input to not pick up the
change. Playwright's fill() handles React controlled inputs correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: filter voice attendance classification to signed-up users only (ROK-707)

Non-signed-up users who briefly joined the voice channel were getting
voice session records classified as no_show, polluting attendance data.
Now classifyEvent() fetches the event's signup list and only classifies
sessions for signed-up users. Orphaned sessions for non-roster members
are deleted during classification.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

public-domain photographs where the image is irreplaceable (Memex,
ENIAC women, Sketchpad, Engelbart mouse), CSS/SVG recreations where
the concept hits harder (Bauhaus grid, SAGE radar, Xerox desktop,
ChatGPT cursor), and interactive JS pieces (Vision Pro eye tracking,
live datacenter watt counter, Amazon friction loop).

Artifacts fade in 0.4s after card text with paradigm-aware treatments:
terminal era gets green CRT filters, GUI era gets warm shadows, all
respect prefers-reduced-motion.

hook instead of directly importing the features object.

commit.message:
fix: simplify isOrgAdmin query to avoid PostgREST join filter issues

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(dashboard): resolve user identity mismatch between internal API and Cognito login

Users created via internal API get synthetic cognitoId (internal-xxx). When the
same user logs in through Cognito/ALB, getCurrentUser() couldn't find them by
cognitoId and the create fallback hit a P2002 unique constraint on email. The
error handler then re-queried by cognitoId which still returned null.

Fix: Add email-based fallback lookup that updates the synthetic cognitoId to the
real Cognito one, unifying the user record. Also fix P2002 handler to check both
cognitoId and email.

commit.message:
fix: remove unsupported --json flag from gh pr create (#311)

`gh pr create` does not support the `--json` flag (unlike `gh pr view`
and `gh pr list`). This caused every draft PR creation to fail with
"unknown flag: --json". The code already had a fallback to parse the
PR URL from stdout, so the fix is to use URL parsing as the primary
path and keep JSON parsing as a future-compatibility fallback.

Closes #308

Co-authored-by: christrudelpw <chris@generacy.ai>

commit.message:
CRITICAL FIX: Fix Netlify deployment configuration for Next.js API routes

🚨 URGENT: API routes not working in production

The issue was incorrect Netlify configuration:
- Removed 'publish = ".next"' setting
- Let @netlify/plugin-nextjs handle deployment automatically
- This should fix API route redirects and enable authentication

🔧 Root Cause:
- Production API routes were redirecting instead of responding
- Auth signup/signin completely broken due to API failure
- Google OAuth and email/password both affected

✅ Expected Fix:
- API routes should now respond correctly
- Authentication endpoints functional
- Admin user login should work

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: auto-bootstrap real data on fresh Render deploy (empty DB)

Render's free tier uses ephemeral disk storage — every deploy wipes the
SQLite database. Previously this caused _deferred_init to fall into demo
mode (generate_sample_data), showing fake data after each push.

New behaviour:
- Production mode is detected automatically when FMP_API_KEY is set in env
  (the Render environment variable already configured)
- On empty DB in production, _bootstrap_real_data() runs automatically:
    1. Loads Nasdaq + NYSE stock universe from SEC (~30s)
    2. Bulk-collects 5 years of Form 4 purchase filings via EFTS (~20-40 min)
    3. Computes forward returns vs SPY
    4. Scores all tickers
- App binds port immediately; dashboard shows empty state while bootstrap
  runs in the background, populating over ~20-40 min
- Override with DEMO_MODE=true to force demo data for local UI testing
- Subsequent starts with non-empty DB skip bootstrap and resume normally

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix timezone mismatch in DB persistence and switch to Langfuse Cloud

Model defaults produced tz-aware datetimes but Postgres columns use
TIMESTAMP WITHOUT TIME ZONE, causing all run persistence to fail.
Also support LANGFUSE_BASE_URL env var and switch docker-compose
to Langfuse Cloud (commented out self-hosted config).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix init container: copy only Mesa-needed libs, not all Alpine libs

Copying all of Alpine's /usr/lib/*.so poisoned LD_LIBRARY_PATH —
Alpine's libcrypto.so.3 shadowed Ubuntu's, breaking Plex's
libpython27 (COMP_get_type symbol removed in Alpine OpenSSL).
Selectively copy only the driver's ldd deps + known runtime deps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
